tasks.description: Section templates [1/2] — data layer: migration (templates table), types, API routes (GET list, POST from section_id, POST deploy, DELETE), client wrappers, STARTER_TEMPLATES constant
acceptance_criteria: New migration 020_section_templates.sql creates `templates` table: id UUID PK default uuid_generate_v4(), workspace_id UUID NOT NULL FK workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, name TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, category TEXT, template_data JSONB NOT NULL, created_by UUID NOT NULL FK users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
RLS policies using can_access_workspace(workspace_id) for SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
update_updated_at trigger on templates table
Index on (workspace_id)
Template interface added to src/types/database.ts with matching fields
GET /api/v1/templates?workspace_id=X returns Template[] — requires workspace_id param, auth check
POST /api/v1/templates with { workspace_id, section_id, name, description?, category? } — fetches section + steps + connections + step_roles, builds JSONB snapshot, creates template row, returns 201
DELETE /api/v1/templates/[id] — auth check, delete, return success

POST /api/v1/templates/[id]/deploy with { tab_id, position_x, position_y } — reads template_data, creates new section + steps + connections with new UUIDs, remaps connection source/target via old-to-new map, matches roles by name (skip unmatched), returns created section with 201
Client wrappers: fetchTemplates(workspaceId), createTemplateFromSection(data), deployTemplate(id, data), deleteTemplate(id)
STARTER_TEMPLATES constant in src/lib/templates.ts — 4 templates per PRD: Customer Onboarding, Support Ticket, Content Creation, Lead Nurturing (each 5 steps)
void logActivity() on template create, deploy, and delete
npx tsc --noEmit passes

patterns_to_follow: API envelope: successResponse(data, 201) / errorResponse(code, message, status) from lib/api/response.ts
RLS: can_access_workspace(workspace_id) for all policies — see migration 019 for latest pattern
Client: apiFetch<T> extracts json.data and returns T directly — do NOT add nested property access
Activity: void logActivity({ supabase, workspace_id, user_id, action, entity_type, entity_id, entity_name })
Migration: uuid_generate_v4() PKs, TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(), update_updated_at() trigger, workspace_id index
Auth: createClient() from lib/supabase/server.ts then supabase.auth.getUser()
Deploy route similar to clone_workspace RPC (migration 018) — old-to-new UUID mapping for connections
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — client wrappers return apiFetch<Type>() directly
void prefix on logActivity() to avoid floating promise warnings
Supabase RLS + INSERT RETURNING — .insert().select() checks SELECT policy

tasks.special_instructions: template_data JSONB shape: { section: { name, summary?, notes?, width?, height? }, steps: [{ name, description?, status?, executor?, time_minutes?, frequency?, cost_per_execution?, position_x, position_y, maturity_current?, maturity_target?, step_type?, roles: [{ role_name: string }] }], connections: [{ source_step_index: number, target_step_index: number, label?: string }] }. Connections use step ARRAY INDICES (not UUIDs) for portability. Deploy remaps indices to new UUIDs. Role assignments store role NAME (not ID) — deploy matches by name in target workspace. STARTER_TEMPLATES use same shape but have no connections or roles — just named steps. Do NOT create PATCH route — PRD specifies GET, POST create, POST deploy, DELETE only.

tasks.description: Disable has_role criteria type in coloring panel dropdown with '(coming soon)' indicator and inline warning for existing has_role rules
acceptance_criteria: has_role option in coloring panel criteria_type select is visually disabled (grayed out, not selectable) with '(coming soon)' text
If existing rules use has_role, show subtle warning: 'Not yet evaluated on canvas' below criteria row
Other 5 criteria types remain fully functional and unaffected
Canvas-view.tsx tint evaluation unchanged
npx tsc --noEmit passes

patterns_to_follow: Radix Select disabled items or native select option disabled={true}
learnings_relevant: has_role criteria is in enum but canvas-view has comment '// Requires additional data fetch' and skips evaluation

tasks.special_instructions: Read coloring-panel.tsx first. Disable has_role in the dropdown, do NOT remove from CriteriaType enum or API validation. Existing has_role rules remain saveable. Also resolves IMP-024.

tasks.description: Replace workspace clone confirm() with Radix Dialog + fix dialog text to list all cloned table types (also resolves IMP-026)
acceptance_criteria: Duplicate Workspace button triggers Radix UI Dialog instead of window.confirm()
Dialog title: 'Duplicate Workspace'
Dialog description: 'A full copy will be created with all canvas data — tabs, sections, steps, connections, teams, roles, people, and tools.'
Cancel button (secondary variant) and Duplicate button (default variant)
Clicking Duplicate triggers existing clone logic with loading state
Clicking Cancel or Escape closes dialog without action
Pattern matches runbook Complete/Cancel dialogs (controlled useState boolean)
Zero window.confirm() calls remain in settings/page.tsx
npx tsc --noEmit passes

patterns_to_follow: Radix Dialog from IMP-012: useState boolean, Dialog/DialogContent/DialogHeader/DialogTitle/DialogDescription/DialogFooter
Button variants: secondary for cancel, default for confirm

tasks.special_instructions: Read runbook-view.tsx for the Radix Dialog pattern from IMP-012. Replicate that pattern. handleClone already exists — wire to dialog confirm button. Also resolves IMP-026 by using accurate clone description.

shared_files.note: FEAT-052 exclusively owns database.ts and client.ts. Other slots must not modify these.
